layman_headline: The crypto market is gripped by extreme fear — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory with a score of just 25 out of 100, and Bitcoin dominance at 56.6% means altcoins are generally struggling. While several coins have bounced in the last 24 hours, the chart structures across nearly all candidates remain bearish — prices are below their key moving averages. The best candidate, Ethereum, has a near-oversold RSI and strong 7-day bounce, but it hasn't reclaimed key resistance levels yet. Rushing into a trade here without confirmation would be catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,800-$1,820 zone and holds that support with increasing volume, that would be a much safer entry point. Don't chase the bounce.
expert_reasoning: None of the swing candidates present a compelling risk/reward entry right now. BONK has a chart bias of -2.0 with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — textbook trend acceleration down, not a place to bottom-fish despite RSI at 31. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but it's already up 25% in 7 days and 51.7% in 5 days — chasing here offers terrible R/R at 0.38x with resistance at $0.40 just 7.4% away. ETH is the most liquid candidate with RSI at 39 (our highest-conviction signal type at 80% win rate), but it's below all major MAs and the chart bias is -0.7. The prudent move is to wait for ETH to either retest $1,800 support or break above the $1,900 resistance with volume confirmation before entering. Extreme Fear at 25 does create opportunity, but the flat trend in fear suggests no capitulation washout has occurred yet.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.6% continues to crush altcoin performance — capital is flowing to Bitcoin, not alts
Extreme Fear has been flat for a week (avg 26.3) suggesting no capitulation bottom yet — market could grind lower
All swing candidates except LDO and 币安人生 are below major moving averages, indicating prevailing downtrends
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a hold here with volume would set up a proper swing entry
Fear & Greed index — a spike down below 20 could signal capitulation and a better entry point
BTC dominance trend — any drop below 55% would signal capital rotating into alts, improving alt swing setups
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC dominance at 56.6%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC itself is bouncing (+4.9% weekly) but altcoin chart structures remain predominantly bearish.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates the market — the best trade right now is no trade until clearer confirmation emerges.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39.4 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but needs price confirmation at support before acting.
Extreme Fear at 25 with a flat weekly trend (26.3 avg) suggests ongoing bearish sentiment without a capitulation flush, making bottom-picking risky.
Bitcoin defying tech stock sell-off and bulls eyeing $70K provides a macro tailwind, but BTC strength at 56.6% dominance is currently hurting alts.
ETH derivatives reset article suggests positioning is clearing out, which could set the stage for a cleaner directional move once confirmed.
BONK shows Three Black Crows with chart bias -2.0, LDO has R/R of only 0.38x near resistance — no candidate offers a clean high-conviction entry right now.
